rationale: The cited work is not only listed as background; it is explicitly identified as the decomposition method used in the managerial analysis. The authors use Van Heerde, Gupta, and Wittink (2003) to split incremental promotion volume into switching from competing brands versus within-brand contract cannibalization, and they report results based on that split. This gives the citation a clear analytical function, even though it supports one section rather than the core structural model.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Van Heerde et al.'s unit sales decomposition to partition simulated promotional lift into competitor-brand gains and own-brand cannibalization across contracts. This decomposition is used to evaluate and compare promotion types such as cash rebates and lease cash.
